test_cases.description: Investigative journalist persona — podcast cover series — editorial illustration style, 4 scenes
test_cases.input_summary: Detailed character description (age, race, hair, build, signature clothing), 4 scenes identified, square and 16:9 formats, illustration style
test_cases.output_summary: Character anchor string includes all required consistency markers; 4 scene prompts use anchor string verbatim; seed workflow explained clearly; --cref flag noted for v6 users; negative prompts applied

test_cases.description: Young male scientist character — educational YouTube series, photorealistic, 3 lab scenes
test_cases.input_summary: Photorealistic style (harder for consistency), 3 lab-setting scenes, character description includes distinctive glasses as key identifier
test_cases.output_summary: Anchor string foregrounds distinctive glasses as primary consistency marker; limitation about photorealistic face drift flagged prominently; --cref workflow emphasized as essential for this style; 3 lab scene prompts vary environment while keeping character anchor identical

test_cases.description: Fantasy character — graphic novel series — illustrated style, 5 scenes including action
test_cases.input_summary: Fantasy/illustrated style, more expressive character design, action scene required (tests consistency beyond portrait), 5 scenes
test_cases.output_summary: Anchor string adapted for illustrated character (includes signature costume elements as key markers); action prompt maintains anchor while varying body position; limitation about action/pose consistency drift noted; negative prompts include style inconsistency markers

notes: The seed and --cref workflow explanation is the most important element of this skill — users who don't know these workflows will get inconsistent results no matter how good the prompt is. The skill correctly flags that character drift is unavoidable and sets appropriate expectations.
